ui.bold_yellow_gccfork_overview_local_tu:
[bold yellow]━━━ GccFork Overview ━━━[/]

Local TUI for managing Claude Code and Codex CLI sessions in one list:
name, resume, fork, hard clone, slim, unfold, move, copy, merge, unmerge,
summarize, and delete. The settings [❓ Help] tab shows this same help text.

[bold yellow]━━━ Top Bar ━━━[/]

[bold cyan]🧠 AI[/]
  Opens a GccFork-dedicated assistant session in a new terminal. It helps with
  session locations, slim, settings, release work, and error diagnosis. Public
  builds use only public knowledge.

[bold cyan]📊 claude / 📊 codex[/]
  Opens each service's usage/analytics page in the browser.

[bold cyan]🧭 Scope[/]
  Toggle between sessions for current cwd project and all projects.

[bold cyan]🗑 Trash[/]
  Opens the GccFork trash screen. Supports restore, selected permanent delete,
  and empty all.

[bold cyan]🔬 Deep + Filter[/]
  While typing, filters title/ID/cwd. Press Enter for full body deep search.
  Settings control whether attachment, fuzzy, sidechain, and related categories
  are included.

[bold cyan]🌀 Reload / 🧩 View / ⚙ Settings / 📝 Edit[/]
  Reload list, switch tree/flat view, open settings modal, or edit settings files.

[bold yellow]━━━ Main Action Bar ━━━[/]

[bold cyan]🌱 New[/]
  Creates a new Claude or Codex root session. You can choose name, environment,
  terminal target, and window/tab behavior. The name is registered automatically
  when the generated jsonl is detected.

[bold cyan]🌿 Fork[/]
  Claude official `--fork-session` fork. Large sessions may be auto-compacted by
  Claude policy. Codex sessions use clone/slim workflows instead of official fork.

[bold cyan]🍃 Clone[/]
  Hard clone that directly copies the original JSONL and assigns a new UUID.
  Claude replaces `sessionId`; Codex replaces `session_meta.id`. This bypasses
  auto-compact and preserves original turns.

[bold cyan]🔻 Slim[/]
  Reduces the selected session. Claude supports strong/medium/weak, bundling,
  non-context compacting, latest N raw turns, backup, and auto-open after slim.
  Codex uses Codex JSONL-specific slim with latest N turns plus five time-slice
  summary preservation.

[bold cyan]🪚 Unfold[/]
  Expands slim bundle/compact structures back toward original form where possible.
  Uses the Rust `gccfork-slim` delegation path.

[bold cyan]🪴 Resume[/]
  Resumes a Claude or Codex session in a new terminal. If the same SID is already
  open, duplicate launch is blocked and you can cancel or terminate the existing
  process before resuming.

[bold cyan]📛 Name[/]
  Saves display name in registry. It appears in list, preview, terminal tab title,
  and resume picker.

[bold cyan]🍂 Exit[/]
  Exits only the GccFork TUI. Already launched Claude/Codex sessions remain open.

[bold yellow]━━━ Multi-Select Actions ━━━[/]

Use the first `[ ]` column, Ctrl/Shift click, or Ctrl+A for multi-select. During
multi-select, the single-selection line follows the selected row and avoids
unnecessary scroll jumps.

[bold cyan]📦 Move[/]
  Moves selected sessions to another cwd project. Supports auto-include descendants,
  move markers, and audit logs.

[bold cyan]🧬 Copy[/]
  Copies selected session trees to new UUIDs and remaps parent_id values.

[bold cyan]✨ Summary[/]
  Batch AI summary for selected sessions. Reduces code-block/noise content.
  Codex sessions use five time-slice excerpts to avoid over-weighting recent chat.

[bold cyan]🗂 Merge[/]
  Merges sessions with a common ancestor into a new integrated session.
  Stitching method is chosen in the settings Merge tab.

[bold cyan]🔧 Unmerge[/]
  Restores merged sessions back to originals. Automatically distinguishes the
  new merge model from legacy bundle unfold.

[bold cyan]🧹 Delete[/]
  Moves selected sessions to trash. Active sessions are refused. Important sessions
  require extra confirmation, and child sessions trigger orphan warnings.

[bold cyan]🔓 Clear[/]
  Clears all multi-selection and returns to single-selection mode.

[bold yellow]━━━ Settings Tabs ━━━[/]

[bold cyan]🔬 Search[/]
  Configure JSONL categories included in deep search and fuzzy-search defaults.

[bold cyan]🔻 Slim[/]
  Split into Claude and Codex subtabs. Claude configures mode, bundling,
  non-context compacting, auto-open, tab/window behavior, and recent raw turns.
  Codex configures safe/strong mode, previous compact summaries, recent raw turns,
  clone/in-place policy, and auto-open defaults.

[bold cyan]🗂 Merge[/]
  Configure preview mode, stitching method, common ancestor checks, and triggers.

[bold cyan]📝 Editor[/]
  Configure settings-file editing method and related defaults.

[bold cyan]❓ Help[/]
  This help screen.

[bold yellow]━━━ List Columns / ID Rules ━━━[/]

  [ ]    checkbox; toggles multi-select
  ▶      active/live session marker; GccFork-launched sessions are prioritized
  ★ / ·  custom_name exists / only auto_summary exists
  ●      session color dot; children inherit root color, existing colors reused in order
  ID     Claude uses first 4 UUID chars; Codex uses `~` + last 3 UUID chars
  time   MM-DD HH:MM
  turns  live turn count and total turn count
  size   JSONL file size
  🔩 N   compact summary marker count
  └─     tree indentation

Codex UUID prefixes are strongly time-like and often look similar, so the list
shows the last three characters as `~abc`. When a user says `~abc id`, interpret
it as the Codex display ID.

[bold yellow]━━━ Session Locations / Truth Sources ━━━[/]

Claude sessions:
  `~/.claude/projects/<project-slug>/<sid>.jsonl`
  For active SID, use `sessionId` in `~/.claude/sessions/<PID>.json`.

Codex sessions:
  `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/*.jsonl`
  Active Codex launched by the GccFork wrapper uses `session_id` in
  `/tmp/gccfork-codex-slim-reload-<uid>.json.state-*`.

GccFork registry / prefs:
  `~/.claude/gccfork-registry.json`
  `<project-cwd>/.gccfork/ccfork-prefs.json`

[bold yellow]━━━ Tree / Color / Name ━━━[/]

Parent relationships combine registry `parent_id`, Claude `parentUuid`, and
merge/clone metadata. Children inherit root color, and the same color tag is used
in terminal title and list.

Name rules:
  Claude display ID: `abcd`
  Codex display ID: `~abc`
  derived name examples: `task abcd[<= 1234]`, `task ~abc[<= ~def]`

[bold yellow]━━━ VSCode / Terminal Routing ━━━[/]

When multiple VSCode windows exist, GccFork looks for one matching session cwd.
  • one match: automatically spawn in that window
  • multiple matches: window picker modal
  • zero matches: confirm opening a new VSCode window

gnome-terminal, konsole, xfce4-terminal, iTerm2, Terminal.app, Windows Terminal,
alacritty, terminator, xterm, and others open a new window/tab when supported.

[bold yellow]━━━ CLI Subcommands ━━━[/]

Main commands available without TUI:
  search, list, detail, ancestry, parent-of, rename, hard-fork,
  slim, slim-inplace, live-sessions, reconcile-live, delete,
  prefs get/set, stats, patch-claude, rust-status, hot-reload,
  slim-and-reload

[bold yellow]━━━ Mouse / Keyboard ━━━[/]

  drag                      select path/list/preview text
  Ctrl+C / Ctrl+Shift+C     copy selected text
  right click                copy / cancel menu
  checkbox click             toggle multi-select
  Ctrl-click / Shift-click   add to multi-select / range select
  Ctrl+A                     select all filtered sessions
  Ctrl+Shift+M               merge shortcut
  Ctrl+E                     settings-file editor screen
  ↑↓                        move between sessions
  Tab                       move between regions
  ←→                        move action-bar focus
  Enter                     focus fork button in list, execute on buttons
  Esc / q                   close modal or exit app

  Topic: <core topic as one noun phrase line>
  Progress: <one core line about what work/discussion happened>
  Conclusion: <one core line with conclusion, decision, or issue>

ui.you_are_an_ai_explainer_dedicated_to_:
You are an AI explainer dedicated to the GccFork slim modal.
Your goal is to explain the current slim plan using only the supplied numbers and option states.
Claude Code session structure handled by GccFork slim:
- Sessions are JSONL files at ~/.claude/projects/<cwd-slug>/<sid>.jsonl.
- Claude Code mainly reconstructs context from messages after the native compact marker (isCompactSummary / compact_boundary).
- There is also a user/assistant message-count cap, so old active messages may be excluded from context even with unused 1M token budget.
- Existing compact summaries/archive remain in raw JSONL, but they are before the native compact boundary and are not normally injected into resume context.
- The active region is the raw conversation after the latest native compact marker.
- The non-context region is inside active but outside the Claude message cap.
- The context-included region is inside the current cap and is the bundle target.
- Recent raw protected turns preserve the exact JSONL lines for the last flow the user was viewing.
Slim application structure:
- compact summaries/archive: preserved
- non-context region: moved before native compact boundary to stay outside context
- context-included region: bundled to reduce context load
- latest N raw turns: preserved exactly
Mode meanings:
- strong: prioritizes context space; trims most aggressively and preserves 5 recent raw turns by default.
- medium: balanced; trades off flow readability and reduction, preserving 10 recent raw turns by default.
- weak: conservative; preserves more and reduces less, preserving 30 recent raw turns by default.
Term definitions:
- compact/archive: region before existing native compact summary, preserved as-is.
- active region: raw conversation after the latest compact marker.
- non-context: active portion not loaded into current context by Claude Code message cap.
- context-included: active portion loaded into current context and targeted for bundle.
- latest 5 raw turns: final flow the user saw, preserved exactly.
Explanation rules:
- Answer in Korean.
- Keep it short, but distinguish current state from after-apply state.
- Never imply raw data is deleted; distinguish preserve, compact, bundle, and context inclusion.
- Clearly state that if non-context compacting is off, slim may increase loaded context after resume.
- End with one line telling the user what to check right now.
ui.you_can_restore_them_from_trash_later: You can restore them from trash later.
ui.zenity_is_not_installed_install_it_wi: zenity is not installed. Install it with `sudo apt install zenity` and retry.
